title: configure-kind-oidc-issuer
summary: Confirmed and re-validated Task 01's decision that no kind config template change is required for the VSO cluster issuer/JWKS (default kubeadm issuer + `jwks_url`/`bound_issuer` strategy remains sufficient); added explanatory-only comments, a new decision/evidence doc, and a locking unit test rather than any functional kind config change.
keyLearnings: - Per Phase 1's decision and this task's own "skip file changes if sufficient" note, no `service-account-issuer`/`service-account-jwks-uri` kubeadm API server args were added to either kind template — re-verified live (fresh `vso-demo` token `iss`, discovery+JWKS reachability from `vault-0` via `SSL_CERT_FILE`, and confirmed `oidc-discovery-reader` RBAC is still applied on `kind-vso-lab`) and all evidence matches the Task 01 spike exactly.

- New unit test asserts the *absence* of issuer/JWKS override keys (anchored regex on actual YAML/arg syntax, not prose) plus presence of the certSANs/apiServerPort config the `jwks_url` reachability plan actually depends on — this locks the decision in so a future change can't silently reintroduce issuer complexity without updating docs.
- Ran all 10 `scripts/tests/test-*.sh` suites after edits; all pass (no regressions from the comment-only edits).

- Observed that Task 03's env-default work (`scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, plan doc Phase 3 status) was already present in the working tree when this task started — downstream tasks should `git status`/diff before assuming a clean baseline, since task execution order/parallelism can leave later work already done.
- The `oidc-discovery-reader` RBAC gap flagged by Task 01 remains unresolved and is explicitly out of scope here — still deferred to task 04/05 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`).
blockers: none — all acceptance/validation criteria for this task were met and re-verified against the live two-cluster environment.
